description: In March 2021, several European nations suspended the use of the AstraZeneca COVID-19 vaccine over reports of rare blood clots. The controversy forced health agencies to communicate complex risk-benefit ratios to a panicked public.

key_events.description: Denmark and Norway pause vaccinations after reports of cerebral venous sinus thrombosis.
EMA declares the vaccine safe, but public skepticism remains high.
Countries start reinstating the vaccine with age-based restrictions.
Global health agencies publish definitive peer-reviewed risk analysis.

calibration_notes: Measures how rapid safety alerts impact public confidence even when scientific consensus favors the treatment.
